what_was_learned: Route/static asset blocker is resolved; runtime UI target is now valid (HTTP 200) and complete (HTML/CSS/JS/API truth).
The first valid full-runtime FPS baseline is now trustworthy and exposes real performance blockers.
Performance blockers are concentrated in FPS metrics, not in route/assets/API correctness.
why_route_fix_was_successful: Native route discovery aligned browser target with server mount, eliminating 404 and restoring required asset truth.
why_valid_fps_blocks_visual_construction: Visual construction cannot be admitted while valid baseline shows BLOCKED average_fps and BLOCKED fps_1pct_low against official budget.

recommended_next_tasks.reason: Highest priority: isolate the real source of low FPS using targeted evidence slices now that measurement is valid.
Second priority: classify asset weight/cost contribution and identify budget hotspots for subsequent optimization scope.
Third priority: continue whole-repo tool absorption without conflating it with runtime optimization execution.

task_order_reasoning: Source-map first to reduce guesswork, asset classification second to bind optimization scope, Scriptorium absorption third to preserve system momentum in parallel governance track.
control_center_readiness_feed: Route/status health should be marked RESOLVED.
Performance acceptance should be marked BLOCKED_BY_FPS_BUDGET.
Next-task queue should prioritize blocker-source-map and asset-classification tasks.
